body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: partial
irrlicht_observes: no

## Scenario primitive

The canonical primitive for `session-resume` is `session_id_assignment`: the agent carries a STABLE session ID across PID lifetimes (via a `--resume` flag or equivalent), and the user-observable signal is that the SAME `session_id` row reappears after relaunch under a new PID, with state/history preserved.

## agent_supports: partial

Aider (verified against installed 0.86.2 source) has NO session-id concept at all. A full grep of the package for `session.?id` / `--resume` returns nothing relevant. Its only resume-adjacent mechanism is `--restore-chat-history` (a boolean flag, `args.py:289`) which, on relaunch in the same git root, replays the messages logged in the per-project `.aider.chat.history.md` (`args.py:274-294`) back into the LLM context.

This preserves the *state and history* half of the user-observable signal — re-launching in the same project picks up the prior conversation — but it is closer to "append to / re-read an existing log" than to true session resume by stable ID. Because the conversational continuity is genuinely there but the defining primitive (a stable session ID) is concretely absent, the honest verdict is `partial` with the carve-out named, not `no`. This matches aider's `capabilities.json`, which already records `session_id_assignment: false` and `session_resume: false`.

## irrlicht_observes: no

This is the load-bearing finding, and it is grounded in the adapter transport, not in aider's docs.

Aider is the sole `FilesUnderCWD` adapter (`agent.go:45`): its transcript is `.aider.chat.history.md` inside each project CWD, and there is no session UUID in the transport. The daemon's process scanner mints the irrlicht `session_id` as `proc-<pid>` — `sessionID := fmt.Sprintf("proc-%d", pid)` (`scanner.go:282`) — and the CWD-resident transcript probe reuses that same `proc.sessionID` (`scanner.go:333`). Identity is therefore keyed on the PID, never on the transcript file and never on any aider-emitted session ID.

Consequence: when aider exits and is re-launched in the same CWD (even with `--restore-chat-history`), the new process gets a NEW PID, hence a NEW `proc-<pid>` session_id, hence a NEW session row. Re-reading the same `.aider.chat.history.md` does not cause irrlicht to reuse the prior `session_id`. The central user-observable signal of this scenario — "the same `session_id` row reappears after relaunch" — cannot occur for aider as the daemon exists today. No `events.jsonl` event would prove it, because there is no event that re-binds an old session_id to a new PID for this adapter.

Note the second-PID-lifetime half of the signal ("each new PID lifetime drives its own state transitions correctly") IS observable — the relaunched process produces its own clean `ready → working → ready` arc via the parser's `####`/`> Tokens:`/IdleFlush state machine. But it does so as a distinct session row, which is the opposite of what the scenario asserts.

## applicable: n/a

The agent has a partial form of the capability (history restore), but irrlicht cannot observe the scenario's defining signal (single continuous session row across PID lifetimes). There is no recording to make: any recording would show two separate `proc-<pid>` rows, which would not demonstrate session-resume. The cell is frozen at the transport layer.

What would unblock it: aider gaining a stable session-id concept AND the aider adapter gaining a CWD-or-transcript-keyed (rather than PID-keyed) identity so a relaunch re-binds the prior session_id. Both are out of scope of a Stage-1 re-assessment.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: aider's relaunch DOES restore conversational state via --restore-chat-history + the per-project .aider.chat.history.md, so the 'history preserved' half of the signal holds at the agent level — but irrlicht surfaces it as a brand-new session row, not the original one.
Identity for aider is PID-derived (proc-<pid>), so any two aider runs in the same CWD are always distinct irrlicht sessions; this is inherent to the FilesUnderCWD transport, not a parser gap.

sources.note: line 282: sessionID := fmt.Sprintf("proc-%d", pid) — aider session_id is PID-derived; line 333 reuses proc.sessionID for the CWD transcript probe, so a relaunch under a new PID is a new row
Source: FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md"} — per-CWD transcript, no session UUID in transport
Parser has no session_id concept; maps markdown lines to events only, with IdleFlush synthesizing turn_done — so each PID lifetime gets its own ready→working→ready arc
session_id_assignment: false and session_resume: false — consistent with partial agent support and no stable-ID primitive

installed aider 0.86.2: only resume-adjacent flag is --restore-chat-history (line 289, boolean) re-reading .aider.chat.history.md (line 274); a full source grep for session.?id / --resume returns nothing — aider has no session-id concept
